heading: Beneficiaries’ rights in mental competence determinations
label: 5501A Beneficiaries’ rights in mental competence determinations
notes: Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–255, div. B, title XIV, § 14017(c)130 Stat. 1307Dec. 13, 2016“, United States Code, as added by subsection (a), shall apply to determinations made by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs on or after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Section 5501A of title 38Dec. 13, 2016
amendments: Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–255, div. B, title XIV, § 14017(c)130 Stat. 1307Dec. 13, 2016“, United States Code, as added by subsection (a), shall apply to determinations made by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs on or after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Section 5501A of title 38Dec. 13, 2016

children.text: Notice of the proposed adverse determination and the supporting evidence.
An opportunity to request a hearing.

An opportunity to present evidence, including an opinion from a medical professional or other person, on the capacity of the beneficiary to manage monetary benefits paid to or for the beneficiary by the Secretary under this title.

An opportunity to be represented at no expense to the Government (including by counsel) at any such hearing and to bring a medical professional or other person to provide relevant testimony at any such hearing.
